steps.title: Trace the evidence
steps.content: North American studies show a positive association between dissolved organic carbon and mercury. Porvari and Verta (Finland) didn't find it, prompting the hypothesis that the link is North America-specific. But most other non-North-American studies (e.g., Luo in China) reproduced the positive association; Porvari and Verta's result is the outlier.

steps.title: Pick the supported conclusion
steps.content:
The accumulated evidence implies the association is not North America-specific — it's general. So dissolved organic carbon and mercury levels typically rise and fall together in fresh water. That's choice C.

A (Finland levels are higher) is unsupported. B contradicts the new evidence. D singles out Luo's study as flawed without basis.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
